Header file for controlling Bluetooth state, including airplane mode and override settings.

# Purpose
This C header file defines an interface for controlling Bluetooth functionality, including airplane mode and Bluetooth stack states. It includes an enumeration `BtCtlModeOverride` to specify override modes for Bluetooth operation. The file declares several functions: [`bt_ctl_init`](<#bt_ctl_init>) initializes the Bluetooth control system, [`bt_ctl_is_airplane_mode_on`](<#bt_ctl_is_airplane_mode_on>) checks if airplane mode is active, [`bt_ctl_is_bluetooth_active`](<#bt_ctl_is_bluetooth_active>) and [`bt_ctl_is_bluetooth_running`](<#bt_ctl_is_bluetooth_running>) determine the operational state of the Bluetooth stack. Additionally, it provides functions to set various flags: [`bt_ctl_set_airplane_mode_async`](<#bt_ctl_set_airplane_mode_async>) for airplane mode, [`bt_ctl_set_enabled`](<#bt_ctl_set_enabled>) for enabling Bluetooth, and [`bt_ctl_set_override_mode`](<#bt_ctl_set_override_mode>) for setting override modes. The function [`bt_ctl_reset_bluetooth`](<#bt_ctl_reset_bluetooth>) resets the Bluetooth stack by stopping and starting communication.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdbool.h`


# Data Structures

---
### BtCtlModeOverride
- **Type**: ``enum``
- **Members**:
    - ``BtCtlModeOverrideNone``: Represents no override mode.
    - ``BtCtlModeOverrideStop``: Represents an override mode to stop the Bluetooth control.
    - ``BtCtlModeOverrideRun``: Represents an override mode to run the Bluetooth control.
- **Description**: Defines the override modes for Bluetooth control, allowing the system to specify whether to stop, run, or have no override on the Bluetooth operation.


# Function Declarations (Public API)

### bt\_ctl\_init<!-- {{#callable_declaration:bt_ctl_init}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/bluetooth/bluetooth_ctl.h#L27>)

Initializes the Bluetooth control system.
- **Description**: Use this function to initialize the Bluetooth control system before any other Bluetooth operations. It sets up necessary resources and state, such as creating a mutex for communication state changes and retrieving the airplane mode status from persistent storage. It also starts a stopwatch for analytics purposes and subscribes to GATT client events. This function must be called once before using other Bluetooth control functions to ensure proper operation.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`bt_ctl_init`](<bluetooth_ctl.c.md#bt_ctl_init>)  (Implementation)


---
### bt\_ctl\_is\_bluetooth\_active<!-- {{#callable_declaration:bt_ctl_is_bluetooth_active}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/bluetooth/bluetooth_ctl.h#L34>)

Checks if the Bluetooth stack is intended to be active.
- **Description**: Use this function to determine if the Bluetooth stack is expected to be operational. It considers the current communication enable state, override mode, and airplane mode status. This function does not guarantee that the Bluetooth stack is fully operational, as it might still be initializing or resetting. Call this function when you need to verify the intended state of the Bluetooth stack, especially in scenarios where the stack's readiness is critical.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns `true` if the Bluetooth stack is intended to be active, otherwise returns `false`.
- **See Also**: [`bt_ctl_is_bluetooth_active`](<bluetooth_ctl.c.md#bt_ctl_is_bluetooth_active>)  (Implementation)


---
### bt\_ctl\_set\_airplane\_mode\_async<!-- {{#callable_declaration:bt_ctl_set_airplane_mode_async}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/bluetooth/bluetooth_ctl.h#L43>)

Sets the airplane mode flag asynchronously.
- **Description**: Use this function to enable or disable airplane mode for the Bluetooth stack asynchronously. Ensure that the Bluetooth system is initialized before calling this function, as it will not execute if initialization has not occurred. This function schedules a state change evaluation if one is not already scheduled, ensuring that the system processes the change in airplane mode status. The change in state is persistent across reboots.
- **Inputs**:
    - `enabled`: A boolean value where `true` enables airplane mode and `false` disables it. The caller retains ownership of this parameter.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`bt_ctl_set_airplane_mode_async`](<bluetooth_ctl.c.md#bt_ctl_set_airplane_mode_async>)  (Implementation)


---
### bt\_ctl\_set\_enabled<!-- {{#callable_declaration:bt_ctl_set_enabled}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/bluetooth/bluetooth_ctl.h#L46>)

Sets the enable flag for the Bluetooth system.
- **Description**: Use this function to enable or disable the Bluetooth system. It must be called only after the Bluetooth system has been initialized. If the system is not initialized, the function logs an error and returns without making any changes. This function is typically used in conjunction with the runlevel system to control the operational state of the Bluetooth stack.
- **Inputs**:
    - `enabled`: A boolean value indicating whether to enable (true) or disable (false) the Bluetooth system. The caller retains ownership of this parameter.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`bt_ctl_set_enabled`](<bluetooth_ctl.c.md#bt_ctl_set_enabled>)  (Implementation)


---
### bt\_ctl\_set\_override\_mode<!-- {{#callable_declaration:bt_ctl_set_override_mode}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/bluetooth/bluetooth_ctl.h#L49>)

Sets the override mode for Bluetooth operation.
- **Description**: Use this function to control the Bluetooth operation mode independently of the airplane mode setting. It must be called only after the Bluetooth system is initialized. If the system is not initialized, the function logs an error and returns without making any changes. This function is useful for overriding the current Bluetooth state, allowing you to stop or start Bluetooth regardless of other settings.
- **Inputs**:
    - `override`: Specifies the desired override mode for Bluetooth operation. It must be one of the values from the `BtCtlModeOverride` enumeration: `BtCtlModeOverrideNone`, `BtCtlModeOverrideStop`, or `BtCtlModeOverrideRun`. The caller retains ownership of this parameter.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`bt_ctl_set_override_mode`](<bluetooth_ctl.c.md#bt_ctl_set_override_mode>)  (Implementation)


---
### bt\_ctl\_reset\_bluetooth<!-- {{#callable_declaration:bt_ctl_reset_bluetooth}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/bluetooth/bluetooth_ctl.h#L52>)

Resets the Bluetooth stack if it is active.
- **Description**: Use this function to reset the Bluetooth stack when it is currently active. This function checks if the Bluetooth stack is active and, if so, schedules a reset operation. If the Bluetooth stack is not active, the function logs a debug message and does not perform a reset. Ensure that the Bluetooth stack is active before calling this function to avoid unnecessary operations.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`bt_ctl_reset_bluetooth`](<bluetooth_ctl.c.md#bt_ctl_reset_bluetooth>)  (Implementation)
